text: Electron affinity is the energy change when:
options: A neutral atom loses an electron
A neutral gaseous atom gains one electron
explanation: Electron affinity (EA): energy released when a neutral gaseous atom gains an electron to form anion. Cl has highest EA.
name: Electron Affinity
subject: che

text: The value of \( \cosh 0 = \)
name: cosh(0)
subject: maths

text: The intercept form of a plane is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1 \), where \(a,b,c\) are x,y,z intercepts.
name: Plane Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The specific heat ratio \(\gamma\) for a diatomic gas (N₂, O₂) is:
name: γ for Diatomic Gas
subject: phy

text: A gun recoils when fired. This is explained by:
explanation: Before firing: total momentum = 0. After: bullet moves forward, gun recoils backward. Total momentum still = 0. Conservation of momentum.
name: Gun Recoil
subject: phy

text: Molarity is defined as:
options: Moles of solute per kg of solvent
Moles of solute per litre of solution
Mass of solute per litre of solution
explanation: Molarity \( M = \frac{\text{moles of solute}}{\text{volume of solution in litres}} \)
name: Molarity Definition
subject: che

text: The rectangular hyperbola has eccentricity:
explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: The electric field \(\vec{E}\) at a point is defined as:
options: Force on any charge placed there
Force per unit positive test charge: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \)
explanation: Electric field: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \) (force per unit positive test charge). Units: N/C = V/m.
name: Definition of Electric Field
subject: phy

text: A function \(f\) is continuous at \(x=a\) if:
explanation: Continuity at \(x=a\): (1) \(f(a)\) defined, (2) limit exists, (3) \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a) \).
name: Continuity Definition
subject: maths

text: In \( \frac{3x+5}{(x-1)(x+2)} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+2} \), the value of \( A \) is:
explanation: Set \(x=1\): \( 3+5 = A(3) \Rightarrow A = \frac{8}{3} \)
name: Finding Constant A
subject: maths

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The Finkelstein reaction involves exchange of halides using:
explanation: Finkelstein reaction: R–Cl + NaI (acetone) → R–I + NaCl. NaCl precipitates (insoluble in acetone), driving equilibrium forward.
name: Finkelstein Reaction
subject: che

text: The most stable oxidation state of iron (Fe) is:
explanation: Fe shows +2 (Fe²⁺, ferrous) and +3 (Fe³⁺, ferric). Fe³⁺ ([Ar]3d⁵) is particularly stable (half-filled d). Both are common and stable.
name: Stable Oxidation States of Fe
subject: che

text: In an isochoric (constant volume) process, work done is:
explanation: Isochoric: \( \Delta V = 0 \Rightarrow W = 0 \). All heat supplied goes to internal energy: \( Q = \Delta U = nC_v\Delta T \).
name: Isochoric Process Work
subject: phy

text: Choke coil (used to control AC without power loss) has:
options: Low resistance, high inductance (high reactance, low power factor)
explanation: Choke coil: high \(L\), low \(R\). High \(X_L = \omega L\) limits AC current with minimal heat loss. Power factor ≈ 0, very efficient AC current controller.
name: Choke Coil
subject: phy

text: The process used to extract iron in a blast furnace uses which reducing agent?
explanation: In blast furnace: C burns to form CO, which reduces iron oxide: \( \text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{CO} \to 2\text{Fe} + 3\text{CO}_2 \). Both C and CO act as reducing agents.
name: Blast Furnace Reducing Agent
subject: che

text: The fundamental frequency of an open pipe of length \(L\) is:
explanation: Open pipe: antinodes at both ends. \( \lambda_1 = 2L \Rightarrow f_1 = v/\lambda_1 = v/2L \). All harmonics present.
name: Open Pipe Fundamental Frequency
subject: phy

text: Heavy water (D₂O) differs from ordinary water in having:
options: Higher density and higher boiling point
Lower density and lower boiling point
explanation: D₂O: density 1.11 g/mL (vs 1.00 for H₂O), bp 101.4°C (vs 100°C), mp 3.8°C (vs 0°C). Heavier due to deuterium.
name: Heavy Water Properties
subject: che

text: The radius of the nth Bohr orbit of hydrogen is:
explanation: Bohr radius formula: \( r_n = 0.529n^2 \) Å (for H). Ground state radius \( a_0 = 0.529 \) Å.
name: Bohr Radius
subject: che

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The period of \( f(x) = \tan x \) is:
explanation: \( \tan(x+\pi) = \tan x \) for all \(x\). The period is \(\pi\).
name: Period of Tangent
subject: maths

text: Newton's law of universal gravitation states: \( F = \)
explanation: Newton's gravitation: \( F = \frac{Gm_1m_2}{r^2} \), where \( G = 6.67\times10^{-11}\,\text{N m}^2/\text{kg}^2 \).
name: Newton's Law of Gravitation
subject: phy

text: The hybridisation of carbon in \( \text{BF}_3 \) (Boron, not Carbon) is:
explanation: Boron in \(\text{BF}_3\): 3 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^2\) hybridisation → trigonal planar.
name: Hybridisation in BF₃
subject: che

text: The equation \( 2\cos^2\theta - \cos\theta - 1 = 0 \) in \([0, 2\pi]\) — the number of solutions is:
explanation: Factor: \( (2\cos\theta+1)(\cos\theta-1) = 0 \Rightarrow \cos\theta = -\frac{1}{2} \) (gives \(\frac{2\pi}{3}, \frac{4\pi}{3}\)) or \(\cos\theta=1\) (gives \(0\)). Total: 3 solutions.
name: Quadratic in cosθ
subject: maths

text: The magnification produced by a lens is:
explanation: Lens magnification: \( m = v/u \). Positive: upright (virtual) image. Negative: inverted (real) image. Mirror: \( m = -v/u \).
name: Lens Magnification
subject: phy

text: The correct order of electromagnetic spectrum from lowest to highest frequency is:
options: Gamma > X-ray > UV > Visible > IR > Microwave > Radio
Radio < Microwave < IR < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma
UV < Visible < IR < Microwave < Radio < X-ray < Gamma
X-ray > Visible > Radio > Gamma > IR > Microwave
explanation: EM spectrum (increasing frequency): Radio < Microwave < Infrared < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma rays.
name: EM Spectrum Order
subject: phy

text: The Sandwich theorem (Squeeze theorem) states: if \( g(x)\leq f(x)\leq h(x) \) and \( \lim g = \lim h = L \), then:
explanation: Squeeze Theorem: if \(f\) is squeezed between \(g\) and \(h\), and both converge to \(L\), then \(\lim f = L\).
name: Squeeze Theorem
subject: maths

text: The DE whose solution is \( y = Ae^{2x}+Be^{-2x} \) is:
explanation: \( y'' = 4Ae^{2x}+4Be^{-2x} = 4y \Rightarrow y''-4y=0 \)
name: DE from Solution
subject: maths

text: In an equilateral triangle with side \(a\), the area is:
explanation: For equilateral triangle: \( \Delta = \frac{1}{2}a\cdot a\sin 60° = \frac{a^2\sqrt{3}}{4} \)
name: Equilateral Triangle Area
subject: maths

text: Heisenberg's uncertainty principle states:
explanation: Heisenberg: \( \Delta x \cdot \Delta p \geq \frac{h}{4\pi} \). Cannot simultaneously know exact position and momentum.
name: Heisenberg Uncertainty
subject: che

text: If \( \omega \) is a cube root of unity, then \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = \)
explanation: The sum of all cube roots of unity is always 0: \( 1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0 \)
name: Cube Roots of Unity
subject: maths

text: The standard enthalpy of formation (\( \Delta H^\circ_f \)) of an element in its standard state is:
explanation: By definition, \( \Delta H^\circ_f = 0 \) for any element in its standard state (e.g., O₂(g), C(graphite)).
name: Standard Enthalpy of Element
subject: che

text: The basicity of aniline (PhNH₂) is much less than aliphatic amines because:
options: Lone pair on N is delocalised into the aromatic ring (resonance), making it less available
N has no lone pair in aniline
explanation: In aniline: N lone pair delocalised into aromatic π system via resonance → less available for protonation. pKb(aniline) ≈ 9.4 vs pKb(CH₃NH₂) ≈ 3.4.
name: Aniline Low Basicity
subject: che

text: The sphere with center \((a,b,c)\) and radius \(r\) has equation:
explanation: Sphere equation: \( (x-a)^2+(y-b)^2+(z-c)^2=r^2 \)
name: Equation of Sphere
subject: maths

text: Tyndall effect is shown by:
explanation: Tyndall effect: scattering of light by colloidal particles, making the path of light visible. True solutions don't show it.
name: Tyndall Effect
subject: che

text: Ozone (O₃) is a better oxidising agent than O₂ because:
explanation: O₃ → O₂ + [O]. Nascent oxygen [O] is highly reactive oxidising agent. This explains O₃'s disinfecting and bleaching ability.
name: Ozone as Oxidising Agent
subject: che

text: Total internal reflection occurs when:
options: Angle of incidence > critical angle AND light travels from denser to rarer medium
Light travels from rarer to denser medium
explanation: TIR conditions: (1) light goes from denser to rarer medium, (2) angle of incidence > critical angle \(\theta_c = \sin^{-1}(n_2/n_1)\). Used in optical fibres.
name: Total Internal Reflection
subject: phy

text: The number of significant figures in 0.00450 is:
explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. In 0.00450: 4, 5, 0 (trailing zero after decimal) = 3 significant figures.
name: Significant Figures
subject: phy

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: The ozone layer is present in the:
explanation: Ozone layer: in stratosphere (mainly 20–35 km). Absorbs harmful UV-B and UV-C radiation. Depleted by CFCs, NOₓ.
name: Ozone Layer Location
subject: che

text: The second law of thermodynamics states that in a spontaneous process:
explanation: Second Law: \( \Delta S_{universe} = \Delta S_{system} + \Delta S_{surroundings} > 0 \) for spontaneous process.
name: Second Law - Entropy
subject: che

text: Boyle's Law states that at constant temperature:
explanation: Boyle's Law: \( P \propto \frac{1}{V} \) at constant \(T\), i.e., \( P_1V_1 = P_2V_2 \).
name: Boyle's Law
subject: che

text: Vitamins that are fat-soluble are:
explanation: Fat-soluble: A, D, E, K (stored in body fat and liver). Water-soluble: B-complex and C (must be taken regularly). Excess fat-soluble → toxicity possible.
name: Fat-Soluble Vitamins
subject: che

text: The time of flight of a projectile launched with speed \(u\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Time of flight: \( T = \frac{2u\sin\theta}{g} \). At maximum height, vertical velocity = 0, then returns.
name: Time of Flight
subject: phy

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: \( \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \) can also be written as:
explanation: By Euler's formula: \( e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta \)
name: Euler's Formula
subject: maths

text: The Product Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(uv) = \)
explanation: Product Rule: \( (uv)' = u'v + uv' \)
name: Product Rule
subject: maths

text: The eccentricity of a parabola is:
explanation: Eccentricity: circle \(=0\), ellipse \(0<e<1\), parabola \(e=1\), hyperbola \(e>1\).
name: Parabola Eccentricity
subject: maths

text: A catalyst in a chemical equilibrium:
options: Increases rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally
Only increases rate of forward reaction
explanation: A catalyst increases rates of both forward and reverse reactions equally. It does not change K or shift equilibrium position.
name: Catalyst and Equilibrium
subject: che

text: If \( P(n) \): \( 7^n - 3^n \) is divisible by 4, then at \( n=2 \): \( 7^2 - 3^2 = \)
explanation: \( 49 - 9 = 40 = 4 \times 10 \). Divisible by 4. ✓
name: Divisibility Verification
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin 30° = \)
explanation: Standard value: \( \sin 30° = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: sin 30°
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution tanθ=tanα
subject: maths

text: The capacitance of an isolated sphere of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Isolated sphere: \( C = 4\pi\epsilon_0 R \). Earth (R≈6400 km): \( C \approx 711\,\mu\text{F} \).
name: Capacitance of Isolated Sphere
subject: phy

text: A dielectric slab inserted in a capacitor (battery disconnected) causes capacitance to:
explanation: Inserting dielectric (battery disconnected, Q constant): \( C' = KC \), \( V' = V/K \), \( E' = E/K \), \( U' = U/K \) (energy decreases).
name: Effect of Dielectric
subject: phy

text: The condition for roots of \( ax^2+bx+c=0 \) to be reciprocal of each other is:
explanation: If roots are \( \alpha, \frac{1}{\alpha} \), product \( = \frac{c}{a} = 1 \Rightarrow a = c \)
name: Reciprocal Roots Condition
subject: maths

text: Which reagent is used to oxidise primary alcohol to aldehyde without over-oxidising to carboxylic acid?
explanation: PCC (CH₂Cl₂, mild): oxidises 1° alcohol → aldehyde (stops there). Strong oxidants (KMnO₄, K₂Cr₂O₇) → carboxylic acid.
name: Selective Oxidation to Aldehyde
subject: che

text: The slope of line joining \( (2,3) \) and \( (5,9) \) is:
name: Slope of Line
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{f(x)}{(x-a)^2(x-b)} \) is:
explanation: For repeated linear factor \((x-a)^2\), decompose as \( \frac{A}{x-a} + \frac{B}{(x-a)^2} \), plus \( \frac{C}{x-b} \).
name: Repeated Linear Factors
subject: maths

text: Area enclosed between curves \( y=f(x) \) and \( y=g(x) \) where \(f(x)\geq g(x)\) is:
explanation: Area between two curves: \( \int_a^b [f(x)-g(x)]\,dx \) when \(f \geq g\) on \([a,b]\).
name: Area Between Curves
subject: maths

text: The condition for constructive interference between two waves is path difference:
explanation: Constructive interference: path difference \( = 0, \lambda, 2\lambda, ... = n\lambda \). Destructive: \( = \lambda/2, 3\lambda/2, ... = (2n+1)\lambda/2 \).
name: Constructive Interference Condition
subject: phy

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment
subject: che

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: The formula \( \cos A = \frac{b^2 + c^2 - a^2}{2bc} \) is rearrangement of:
explanation: This is a direct rearrangement of the cosine rule \( a^2 = b^2+c^2-2bc\cos A \).
name: Cosine Rule Rearranged
subject: maths

text: Law of conservation of linear momentum holds when:
options: Net external force on the system is zero
All particles move with same velocity
explanation: Linear momentum is conserved when net external force = 0: \( \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} = \vec{F}_{ext} = 0 \Rightarrow \vec{p} = \text{const} \).
name: Conservation of Momentum
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \), then \( \frac{1}{\alpha} + \frac{1}{\beta} = \)
explanation: \( \frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta} = \frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha\beta} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Sum of Reciprocals
subject: maths

text: The photoelectric effect conclusively demonstrated that:
options: Light energy is quantised (comes in packets called photons)
explanation: Photoelectric effect: showed light energy is quantised (\(E = h\nu\)), not continuous. Wave theory couldn't explain the existence of threshold frequency and instantaneous emission.
name: Conclusion from Photoelectric Effect
subject: phy

text: Which of the following has the smallest size?
explanation: All are isoelectronic (10 electrons). Higher nuclear charge → smaller size. Al³⁺ (Z=13) smallest, Na (Z=11) largest among ions.
name: Smallest Isoelectronic Size
subject: che

explanation: \( \sin^{-1} \) is an odd function: \( \sin^{-1}(-x) = -\sin^{-1}(x) \).
name: Odd Function Property
subject: maths

text: The equation \( \tan\theta = -1 \) has principal solution:
explanation: In \((0, 2\pi)\): \( \tan\theta = -1 \) at \( \theta = \frac{3\pi}{4} \) (2nd quad) and \( \frac{7\pi}{4} \) (4th quad). Principal value: \( \frac{3\pi}{4} \).
name: tan = -1 Solution
subject: maths

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: At resonance in an LCR series circuit:
options: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow Z = R \) (minimum impedance, maximum current)
explanation: Resonance: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Impedance = \(R\) (minimum). Current = \(V/R\) (maximum). Power factor = 1.
name: LCR Resonance
subject: phy

text: A wire of length \(L\), cross-section \(A\), Young's modulus \(Y\) is stretched by \(\Delta L\). The force required is:
explanation: \( Y = \frac{FL}{A\Delta L} \Rightarrow F = \frac{YA\Delta L}{L} \). The 'spring constant' of a wire: \( k = YA/L \).
name: Force in Stretched Wire
subject: phy

text: The directrix of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2=4ax \): directrix is the vertical line \( x = -a \).
name: Directrix of Parabola
subject: maths

text: At a local maximum, the second derivative satisfies:
explanation: Second Derivative Test: local max iff \( f'(x)=0 \) and \( f''(x)<0 \).
name: Second Derivative Test - Max
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of coupling between two inductors \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) is:
explanation: Coupling coefficient: \( k = M/\sqrt{L_1L_2} \). \( k=1 \): perfect coupling (all flux links both coils). \( k=0 \): no coupling.
name: Coefficient of Coupling
subject: phy

text: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: FTC: \( \int_a^b f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) \), where \(F'(x)=f(x)\).
name: Fundamental Theorem
subject: maths

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \sqrt{x - 3} \) is:
explanation: For \(\sqrt{x-3}\) to be defined, \( x - 3 \geq 0 \Rightarrow x \geq 3 \)
name: Domain of Square Root
subject: maths

text: In the Arrhenius plot of \(\ln k\) vs \(\frac{1}{T}\), the slope is:
explanation: \( \ln k = \ln A - \frac{E_a}{RT} \). Slope of \(\ln k\) vs \(1/T\) graph \( = -E_a/R \).
name: Arrhenius Plot Slope
subject: che

text: The number of handshakes when 10 people each shake hands with every other is:
explanation: Each handshake involves 2 people: \( ^{10}C_2 = 45 \)
name: Handshakes Problem
subject: maths

text: Which type of solid has the highest melting point?
explanation: Covalent network solids (e.g., diamond, SiO₂) have extremely high melting points due to strong covalent bonds throughout.
name: Highest Melting Point Solid
subject: che

text: If \( A \) is a symmetric matrix, then \( A^T = \)
explanation: A matrix is symmetric if \( A^T = A \) (transpose equals itself).
name: Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: Faraday's first law of electrolysis states:
options: Mass deposited is independent of current
Mass deposited ∝ charge passed \( (m = ZQ = ZIt) \)
explanation: Faraday's First Law: \( m = Z \cdot Q = Z \cdot I \cdot t \), where \(Z\) = electrochemical equivalent.
name: Faraday's First Law
subject: che

text: The product formula \( 2\sin A \cos B = \)
explanation: Product-to-Sum: \( 2\sin A\cos B = \sin(A+B) + \sin(A-B) \)
name: Product-to-Sum
subject: maths

text: Joule's law of heating states heat produced \( H = \)
explanation: Joule's law: \( H = I^2Rt \) (in joules). In calories: \( H = I^2Rt/4.18 \). Basis for electric heaters, fuses.
name: Joule's Law of Heating
subject: phy

text: The electric dipole moment is defined as:
explanation: Electric dipole moment: \( \vec{p} = q\vec{d} \). Direction: from negative to positive charge. Units: C·m.
name: Electric Dipole Moment
subject: phy

text: The equation of line through \((x_1,y_1)\) with slope \(m\) is:
explanation: Point-slope form: \( y - y_1 = m(x - x_1) \)
name: Point-Slope Form
subject: maths

text: Aldehydes and ketones are distinguished by Tollens' reagent (ammoniacal AgNO₃). Aldehydes give:
explanation: Tollens' test: RCHO + 2[Ag(NH₃)₂]⁺ + 2OH⁻ → RCOOH + 2Ag↓ + 4NH₃. Silver mirror formed. Ketones give no reaction.
name: Tollens' Test
subject: che

text: The rank of a \( 3 \times 3 \) identity matrix \( I_3 \) is:
explanation: The identity matrix is full rank. For \( I_3 \): rank \( = 3 \) since \( |I_3| = 1 \neq 0 \).
name: Rank of Identity Matrix
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}dx = \sin^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Arcsin Standard Form
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆
subject: che

text: In the reaction \( 2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \to 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \), 4 g of \( \text{H}_2 \) reacts with how many grams of \( \text{O}_2 \)?
explanation: 4 g \(\text{H}_2\) = 2 mol. From stoichiometry, 2 mol \(\text{H}_2\) needs 1 mol \(\text{O}_2\) = 32 g.
name: Stoichiometry Calculation
subject: che

text: For a compound to be aromatic by Hückel's rule, it must have:
options: (4n+2) π electrons in a planar cyclic conjugated system
An even number of π electrons
explanation: Hückel's rule: aromatic if planar, cyclic, fully conjugated, with (4n+2) π electrons (n = 0,1,2...). Benzene: n=1, 6π electrons.
name: Hückel's Rule
subject: che

text: The integrating factor for \( \frac{dy}{dx} + Py = Q \) is:
explanation: For linear DE \( y'+Py=Q \), integrating factor (IF) \( = e^{\int P\,dx} \).
name: Integrating Factor
subject: maths

text: The escape velocity from Earth is \( v_e = \sqrt{2gR} \approx 11.2\,\text{km/s} \). This is derived from:
options: Kinetic energy = potential energy at surface
Setting total mechanical energy to zero: \( \frac{1}{2}mv_e^2 - \frac{GMm}{R} = 0 \)
explanation: Escape: KE = |gravitational PE|. \( \frac{1}{2}mv_e^2 = \frac{GMm}{R} \Rightarrow v_e = \sqrt{\frac{2GM}{R}} = \sqrt{2gR} \).
name: Escape Velocity Derivation
subject: phy

text: The condition for constructive interference is path difference:
explanation: Constructive interference: \( \Delta = n\lambda \) (n = 0,±1,±2,...). Destructive: \( \Delta = (2n+1)\lambda/2 \).
name: Constructive Interference YDSE
subject: phy

text: Heat transfer by convection occurs primarily in:
explanation: Convection: heat transfer by movement of fluid itself. Natural (density difference) or forced. Cannot occur in solids or vacuum.
name: Convection in Fluids
subject: phy

text: Nylon-6,6 is formed from:
explanation: Nylon-6,6: condensation of hexamethylenediamine (H₂N(CH₂)₆NH₂) + adipic acid (HOOC(CH₂)₄COOH). Numbers refer to carbon atoms in each monomer.
name: Nylon-6,6
subject: che

text: The AM-GM inequality states: for positive reals \(a\) and \(b\):
explanation: AM ≥ GM: \( \frac{a+b}{2} \geq \sqrt{ab} \). Equality holds iff \(a = b\).
name: AM-GM Inequality
subject: maths

text: A p-n junction diode conducts in:
options: Forward bias (forward current flows when V > knee voltage ~0.7V for Si)
explanation: Forward bias: p-side to + terminal. Reduces barrier → significant current flows. Reverse bias: increases barrier → only tiny leakage current.
name: Diode Conduction
subject: phy

text: The number of diagonals of a polygon with \(n\) sides is:
explanation: Total lines \( = \binom{n}{2} \), minus \(n\) sides: diagonals \( = \frac{n(n-1)}{2} - n = \frac{n(n-3)}{2} \)
name: Diagonals of Polygon
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a circle with center \((h,k)\) and radius \(r\) is:
explanation: Standard form: \( (x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2 \). When center is origin: \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \).
name: Standard Circle Equation
subject: maths

text: If a constant \(c\) is added to each observation, the variance:
explanation: Adding a constant shifts all values equally; deviations from mean remain unchanged. Variance is unaffected.
name: Variance under Addition of Constant
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is the correct order of acidic strength of hydrocarbons?
explanation: Acidic strength: alkyne (sp C, more electronegative) > alkene (sp²) > alkane (sp³). pKa: alkyne ≈ 25, alkene ≈ 44, alkane ≈ 50.
name: Acidity of Hydrocarbons
subject: che

text: Bernoulli's equation for steady flow is:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( P + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 + \rho g h = \text{const} \). Energy conservation for ideal fluid flow.
name: Bernoulli's Equation
subject: phy

text: The reaction of Na₂O₂ with cold water gives:
explanation: \( 2\text{Na}_2\text{O}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \to 4\text{NaOH} + \text{O}_2 \). Or with cold dilute acid: \( \text{Na}_2\text{O}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \to \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \).
name: Na₂O₂ and Water
subject: che

text: The reaction of primary aromatic amine with NaNO₂/HCl at 0–5°C gives:
explanation: Diazotisation: ArNH₂ + NaNO₂ + HCl (0–5°C) → ArN₂⁺Cl⁻ (diazonium salt). Must be at low temp (diazonium salts are unstable).
name: Diazotisation
subject: che

text: The acidity of carboxylic acids compared to alcohols is due to:
explanation: RCOOH (pKa ~5) >> ROH (pKa ~16). The carboxylate anion –COO⁻ is resonance-stabilised (negative charge delocalised over 2 oxygens).
name: Acidity of Carboxylic Acids
subject: che

text: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is:
name: Zero Order Half-life
subject: che

text: The transformer works on the principle of:
explanation: Transformer: mutual induction. \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 = I_1/I_2 \) (ideal). Step-up: \(N_2>N_1\). Step-down: \(N_2<N_1\).
name: Transformer Principle
subject: phy

text: The knee voltage (cut-in voltage) for a silicon p-n junction diode is approximately:
explanation: Si diode: knee voltage ≈ 0.7 V. Ge diode: ≈ 0.3 V. Below knee voltage, current is negligible. Si diodes are more common (higher temperature stability).
name: Knee Voltage of Si Diode
subject: phy

text: The term containing \(x^4\) in \( \left(x^2 + \frac{1}{x}\right)^7 \) is:
explanation: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{7}{r}(x^2)^{7-r} x^{-r} = \binom{7}{r} x^{14-3r} \). Set \(14-3r=4 \Rightarrow r=\frac{10}{3}\)... Correct: \(14-3r=4\Rightarrow r=\frac{10}{3}\) — closest is \(T_5\) for \(r=4\), giving \(x^2\). Recheck: \(T_5\) is answer.
name: Finding Specific Term
subject: maths

text: The expression \( x^2 + x + 1 \) is positive for:
explanation: Discriminant \( = 1 - 4 = -3 < 0 \) and \( a = 1 > 0 \), so expression is positive for all real \( x \).
name: Sign of Quadratic Expression
subject: maths

text: The equation of tangent to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Tangent
subject: maths

text: For two circles with centers \( d \) apart and radii \( r_1, r_2 \), they are externally tangent when:
explanation: External tangency: \( d = r_1 + r_2 \). Internal tangency: \( d = |r_1 - r_2| \).
name: External Tangency
subject: maths

text: The greatest binomial coefficient in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
explanation: For even \(n = 10\), greatest coefficient is the middle one: \( \binom{10}{5} = 252 \)
name: Greatest Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: In single slit diffraction, the first minimum occurs when path difference = \(\lambda\), i.e.:
explanation: Single slit diffraction minima: \( a\sin\theta = n\lambda \) (n=1,2,...). Central maximum is the widest and brightest. Width \( = 2\lambda D/a \).
name: Single Slit Diffraction Minima
subject: phy

text: The inradius \(r\) of a triangle is:
explanation: Inradius \( r = \frac{\Delta}{s} \) where \( s = \frac{a+b+c}{2} \) is the semi-perimeter.
name: Inradius Formula
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{3x^2+2x}{5x^2-1} = \)
explanation: Divide numerator and denominator by \(x^2\): \( \frac{3+2/x}{5-1/x^2} \to \frac{3}{5} \)
name: Rational Limit at Infinity
subject: maths

text: In beta-minus (\(\beta^-\)) decay:
options: A proton converts to neutron + positron + neutrino
A neutron converts to proton + electron (\(\beta^-\)) + antineutrino: \( n \to p + e^- + \bar{\nu} \)
explanation: \(\beta^-\) decay: \( n \to p + e^- + \bar{\nu}_e \). A unchanged, Z increases by 1. Example: \( ^{14}_6C \to ^{14}_7N + e^- + \bar{\nu} \).
name: Beta-Minus Decay
subject: phy

text: From a pack of 52 cards, probability of drawing a king is:
explanation: 4 kings in 52 cards: \( P = \frac{4}{52} = \frac{1}{13} \)
name: Card Probability
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \tan 45° = \)
name: Standard Value tan 45
subject: maths

explanation: Standard result: \( \int \frac{1}{1+x^2}\,dx = \tan^{-1}x+C \)
name: Standard Arctan Integral
subject: maths

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property
subject: maths

text: The mean and variance of a data set are 5 and 4. The coefficient of variation is:
name: CV Calculation
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of \( x^5 \) in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
name: Coefficient of x^5
subject: maths

text: The locus of points equidistant from \( (2,0) \) and \( (-2,0) \) is:
explanation: Perpendicular bisector of segment from \((-2,0)\) to \((2,0)\) is the \(y\)-axis: \(x=0\).
name: Locus Equidistant
subject: maths

text: Geometric isomerism (cis-trans) is possible in square planar complexes of type:
explanation: cis-trans isomers in square planar: MA₂B₂ type. E.g., [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂]: cis (cisplatin, anticancer) and trans isomers.
name: Geometric Isomerism
subject: che

text: Conservative forces have the property that work done:
options: Is independent of path (depends only on initial and final positions)
Is always maximum on shortest path
explanation: Conservative force (gravity, spring, electrostatic): work done is path-independent. Non-conservative (friction): path-dependent.
name: Conservative Forces
subject: phy

text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
explanation: Solid sphere (diameter): \( I = \frac{2}{5}MR^2 \). Hollow sphere: \( \frac{2}{3}MR^2 \). Solid disc: \( \frac{1}{2}MR^2 \).
name: MOI of Solid Sphere
subject: phy

text: Reaction of alkyl halides with Mg in dry ether gives:
explanation: Grignard reagent: R–X + Mg → R–MgX (dry ether). RMgX is a powerful nucleophile/base used in organic synthesis.
name: Grignard Reagent
subject: che

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths

text: For a projectile, the horizontal range is maximum when the angle of projection is:
explanation: Range \( R = \frac{u^2\sin 2\theta}{g} \). Maximum when \( \sin 2\theta = 1 \Rightarrow 2\theta = 90° \Rightarrow \theta = 45° \).
name: Maximum Range of Projectile
subject: phy

text: The sum \( 1^2 + 2^2 + \cdots + n^2 = \)
explanation: \( \sum_{k=1}^n k^2 = \frac{n(n+1)(2n+1)}{6} \). Standard formula verified by induction.
name: Sum of Squares
subject: maths

text: Two planes are perpendicular if their normals \( (a_1,b_1,c_1) \) and \( (a_2,b_2,c_2) \) satisfy:
explanation: Planes are perpendicular when their normals are perpendicular: \( a_1a_2+b_1b_2+c_1c_2=0 \)
name: Perpendicular Planes
subject: maths

text: The equation \( x^3 - 1 = 0 \) has how many real roots?
name: Roots of x³=1
subject: maths

text: The number of real roots of \( x^2 + |x| + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: For all real \(x\): \( x^2 \geq 0,\ |x| \geq 0 \), so \( x^2+|x|+1 \geq 1 > 0 \). No real roots.
name: No Real Roots
subject: maths

text: For the Haber process \( \text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NH}_3 \), increasing pressure:
options: Shifts equilibrium to left (more N₂ and H₂)
explanation: Reactants: 4 mol gas; Products: 2 mol gas. Increasing pressure → shift to side with fewer moles of gas → right (more NH₃).
name: Haber Process and Pressure
subject: che

text: The cross product \( \vec{a} \times \vec{b} \) gives a vector:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\times\vec{b} \) is perpendicular to both \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) (normal to their plane).
name: Cross Product Direction
subject: maths

text: By Descartes' rule, the maximum number of positive roots of \( x^3 - 3x^2 + 4 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Sign changes: \(+, -, +\) — 2 sign changes, so max 2 positive roots (or 0).
name: Descartes' Rule of Signs
subject: maths

text: The reaction of HCHO (formaldehyde) with excess NaOH is:
explanation: HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro: 2HCHO + NaOH → CH₃OH (methanol) + HCOONa (sodium formate). Cross-Cannizzaro also possible.
name: HCHO Cannizzaro
subject: che

text: The value of \( \cos 60° = \)
explanation: \( \cos 60° = \frac{1}{2} \). Standard trigonometric value.
name: cos 60°
subject: maths

text: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j} = \)
explanation: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j}=\hat{k},\ \hat{j}\times\hat{k}=\hat{i},\ \hat{k}\times\hat{i}=\hat{j} \). Cyclic order.
name: Unit Vector Cross Products
subject: maths

text: The efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between temperatures \(T_H\) and \(T_C\) is:
explanation: Carnot efficiency: \( \eta = 1 - \frac{T_C}{T_H} = \frac{T_H - T_C}{T_H} \). This is the maximum possible efficiency for any heat engine.
name: Carnot Efficiency
subject: phy

text: For ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), eccentricity \( e = \)
explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The function \( f(x) = |x| \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = |-x| = |x| = f(x) \). So \(f\) is an even function.
name: Even or Odd Function
subject: maths

text: The formula to calculate molar mass from freezing point depression is:
explanation: \( M_2 = \frac{K_f \times w_2 \times 1000}{\Delta T_f \times w_1} \), where \(w_1\) = mass of solvent (g), \(w_2\) = mass of solute (g).
name: Molar Mass from FP Depression
subject: che

text: The kinetic energy of rolling without slipping is:
explanation: Rolling KE = translational + rotational: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + \frac{1}{2}I\omega^2 \). For solid sphere: \( KE = \frac{7}{10}mv^2 \).
name: Rolling Kinetic Energy
subject: phy

text: The approximate value formula using differentials is:
explanation: Linear approximation: \( f(x+\Delta x) \approx f(x) + f'(x)\cdot\Delta x \)
name: Linear Approximation
subject: maths

text: The work done in rotating a magnetic dipole from angle \(\theta_1\) to \(\theta_2\) in field \(B\) is:
explanation: \( U = -MB\cos\theta \). Work: \( W = U_2 - U_1 = -MB\cos\theta_2+MB\cos\theta_1 = MB(\cos\theta_1-\cos\theta_2) \).
name: Work Done on Magnetic Dipole
subject: phy

text: Inductive effect (–I) is the permanent displacement of σ-bond electrons due to electronegativity difference. Which group shows +I effect?
explanation: +I effect (electron-donating by induction): alkyl groups (–CH₃, –C₂H₅). –I effect (electron-withdrawing): –NO₂, –COOH, –Cl, –OH.
name: Inductive Effect
subject: che

text: From 6 men and 4 women, a committee of 3 men and 2 women can be formed in:
name: Committee Formation
subject: maths

text: The Wheatstone bridge is balanced when:
options: Sum of all resistances is zero
explanation: Wheatstone bridge balance: \( \frac{P}{Q} = \frac{R}{S} \) or \( PS = QR \). Used to find unknown resistance accurately.
name: Wheatstone Bridge Balance Condition
subject: phy

text: The gravitational field intensity at a distance \(r\) outside a uniform sphere of mass \(M\) is:
explanation: Outside sphere: \( g = \frac{GM}{r^2} \) (same as point mass). Inside: \( g = \frac{GM}{R^3}r \propto r \).
name: Gravitational Field
subject: phy
